texts.label: text
texts.orig: ii	Clinical Reminders TBI Screening Patch PXRM*2*8	3/20/2007
texts.text: ii	Clinical Reminders TBI Screening Patch PXRM*2*8	3/20/2007

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PXRM*2*8 distributes the new National VA-TBI SCREENING reminder definition and dialog. This reminder has been developed and sponsored by the Office of Patient Care Services.
texts.text: PXRM*2*8 distributes the new National VA-TBI SCREENING reminder definition and dialog. This reminder has been developed and sponsored by the Office of Patient Care Services.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For more detailed information about use of this TBI Screening reminder, please review the PowerPoint at:
texts.text: For more detailed information about use of this TBI Screening reminder, please review the PowerPoint at:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clinical Reminders patch PXRM*2.0*8 adds one new reminder and dialog, along with taxonomies, reminder terms, and health factors to support the TBI project.
texts.text: Clinical Reminders patch PXRM*2.0*8 adds one new reminder and dialog, along with taxonomies, reminder terms, and health factors to support the TBI project.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Data is added to the following files:
texts.text: Data is added to the following files:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch can be loaded with users on the system. Installation will take less than two minutes. Installation should be coordinated with the person who manages Clinical Reminders at your site and be scheduled during a time of lower system usage.
texts.text: This patch can be loaded with users on the system. Installation will take less than two minutes. Installation should be coordinated with the person who manages Clinical Reminders at your site and be scheduled during a time of lower system usage.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This build can be loaded with users on the system. Updating of cross-references for the new data added to the files will occur during the install.
texts.text: This build can be loaded with users on the system. Updating of cross-references for the new data added to the files will occur during the install.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The install needs to be done by a person with DUZ(0) set to "@."
texts.text: The install needs to be done by a person with DUZ(0) set to "@."

texts.text: Use FTP to access the build, PXRM_2_0_8.KID, from one of the following locations:

texts.orig: 2 Install the build first in a training or test account.
texts.text: 2 Install the build first in a training or test account.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.
texts.text: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In programmer mode, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, then the option LOAD a Distribution. Enter your directory name and PXRM_2_0_8.KID at the Host File prompt.
texts.text: In programmer mode, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, then the option LOAD a Distribution. Enter your directory name and PXRM_2_0_8.KID at the Host File prompt.

texts.text: From the Installation menu, you may elect to use the following options:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global. If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.
texts.text: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global. If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again. If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.
texts.text: Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again. If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The post-install routine, POST^PXRMP8I, installs the “packed” components of the VA- TBI SCREENING reminder and VA-TBI SCREENING reminder dialog onto your system, via reminder exchange tools. The reminder exchange entry being installed is called VA- TBI-SCREENING.
texts.text: The post-install routine, POST^PXRMP8I, installs the “packed” components of the VA- TBI SCREENING reminder and VA-TBI SCREENING reminder dialog onto your system, via reminder exchange tools. The reminder exchange entry being installed is called VA- TBI-SCREENING.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After the installation has finished, if you discover that the components weren’t installed correctly for some reason, you can use the Exchange options on the Reminders Manager Menu to install the components of the “packed” reminder.
texts.text: After the installation has finished, if you discover that the components weren’t installed correctly for some reason, you can use the Exchange options on the Reminders Manager Menu to install the components of the “packed” reminder.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The init routine PXRMP8I may be deleted once the installation has completed. Sites should use the Kernel “Delete Routines” option [XTRDEL] to delete this routine.
texts.text: The init routine PXRMP8I may be deleted once the installation has completed. Sites should use the Kernel “Delete Routines” option [XTRDEL] to delete this routine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The TBI reminder is applicable to all patients whose last service separation date is after 9/11/01 and to active duty patients.
texts.text: The TBI reminder is applicable to all patients whose last service separation date is after 9/11/01 and to active duty patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE: This reminder uses the same reminder term that was included in the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder to determine whether active duty patients should be screened or not. The reminder term VA-ACTIVE DUTY is included in this reminder and is available to cause patients to be part of the cohort. This term contains the computed finding
texts.text: NOTE: This reminder uses the same reminder term that was included in the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder to determine whether active duty patients should be screened or not. The reminder term VA-ACTIVE DUTY is included in this reminder and is available to cause patients to be part of the cohort. This term contains the computed finding

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VA-PATIENT TYPE, which can be used to include active duty patients.
texts.text: VA-PATIENT TYPE, which can be used to include active duty patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sites that do not screen active duty patients may remove the computed finding from this reminder term.
texts.text: Sites that do not screen active duty patients may remove the computed finding from this reminder term.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This reminder is resolved if the patient did not serve in OEF/OIF, as recorded by using this reminder dialog or the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN dialog. Therefore, the denominator for this reminder is the same as that used for the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST- DEPLOY SCREEN – this number DOES NOT represent the number of patients who served in OEF/OIF.
texts.text: This reminder is resolved if the patient did not serve in OEF/OIF, as recorded by using this reminder dialog or the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN dialog. Therefore, the denominator for this reminder is the same as that used for the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST- DEPLOY SCREEN – this number DOES NOT represent the number of patients who served in OEF/OIF.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Please note that this reminder and the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder are not constructed to run reports on those patients who actually served in OEF/OIF.
texts.text: Please note that this reminder and the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder are not constructed to run reports on those patients who actually served in OEF/OIF.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NOTE: Some local sites use additional reminders that have more appropriate denominators and look at patients with known OEF/OIF service who need TBI screening, or who screened positive for TBI and need follow-up, or have positive PTSD screens or positive depression screens.
texts.text: NOTE: Some local sites use additional reminders that have more appropriate denominators and look at patients with known OEF/OIF service who need TBI screening, or who screened positive for TBI and need follow-up, or have positive PTSD screens or positive depression screens.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a veteran’s screen is positive, the positive findings should be reviewed with the patient by an appropriately trained clinician and a consult for further evaluation recommended. In general, it is best if the clinician personally completes the screen, particularly as the questions asked are of clinical value. If clinic support staff collects answers to the initial questions on the screen, positive screens must be brought to the attention of the responsible clinician immediately and reviewed with the patient. Consults should not be automatically submitted without discussion between clinician and the patient.
texts.text: When a veteran’s screen is positive, the positive findings should be reviewed with the patient by an appropriately trained clinician and a consult for further evaluation recommended. In general, it is best if the clinician personally completes the screen, particularly as the questions asked are of clinical value. If clinic support staff collects answers to the initial questions on the screen, positive screens must be brought to the attention of the responsible clinician immediately and reviewed with the patient. Consults should not be automatically submitted without discussion between clinician and the patient.

texts.orig: After installing Patch 8, follow the steps below to implement your TBI screening reminder and dialog.
texts.text: After installing Patch 8, follow the steps below to implement your TBI screening reminder and dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following dialog elements are meant to be used for ordering a consult to the team at your facility that does further evaluation of patients with possible TBI.
texts.text: The following dialog elements are meant to be used for ordering a consult to the team at your facility that does further evaluation of patients with possible TBI.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VA-TBI OI CONSULT FOR KNOWN TBI VA-PDIQ POLYTRAUMA CONSULT
texts.text: VA-TBI OI CONSULT FOR KNOWN TBI VA-PDIQ POLYTRAUMA CONSULT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At the time of install, the Reminder Dialog will use the GMRCOR CONSULT finding item as the consult order dialog for both of these dialog elements. Sites should substitute a quick order or an order menu for ordering consults to the TBI team at your facility. If no such service is available yet, then sites may wish to leave the GMRCOR CONSULT in the element until a consult becomes available.
texts.text: At the time of install, the Reminder Dialog will use the GMRCOR CONSULT finding item as the consult order dialog for both of these dialog elements. texts.orig: Reminder is applicable once in a lifetime of all patients whose date of separation from the service is 9/11/01 or later and have had service in OEF/OIF.	If Service Date of Separation is more recent than last TBI Screening, then reminder will be due again for patient.
texts.text: Reminder is applicable once in a lifetime of all patients whose date of separation from the service is 9/11/01 or later and have had service in OEF/OIF.	If Service Date of Separation is more recent than last TBI Screening, then reminder will be due again for patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reminder is resolved by completing the screen.
texts.text: Reminder is resolved by completing the screen.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reminder creation requested by the Office of Patient Care Services. Designed by the TBI Screening Workgroup chaired by Dr. Barbara Sigford and based on a reminder from Minneapolis built by Ronald Patire.
texts.text: Reminder creation requested by the Office of Patient Care Services. Designed by the TBI Screening Workgroup chaired by Dr. Barbara Sigford and based on a reminder from Minneapolis built by Ronald Patire.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patients who served in combat in either Iraq (Operation Iraqi Freedom) or in Afghanistan (Operation Enduring Freedom) should be screened for Traumatic Brain Injury.
texts.text: Patients who served in combat in either Iraq (Operation Iraqi Freedom) or in Afghanistan (Operation Enduring Freedom) should be screened for Traumatic Brain Injury.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patients who were discharged from the service prior to 9/11/01 or who did NOT serve in OEF or OIF do NOT need to be screened for TBI.
texts.text: Patients who were discharged from the service prior to 9/11/01 or who did NOT serve in OEF or OIF do NOT need to be screened for TBI.

texts.text: Default PATIENT COHORT LOGIC to see if the Reminder applies to a patient: (SEX)&(AGE)&FI(1)!FI(2)
